1	# Story 2.3: Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	## Story
8	
9	As a deepwiki user,
10	I want to review a 10-line summary of the auto-generated brief before CS runs,
11	so that I can approve, edit, or reject it — and rejection falls back to interactive BS with pre-populated fields.
12	
13	## Acceptance Criteria
14	
15	1. **Given** BS auto-brief has been generated, **When** the validation step presents the summary to the user, **Then** the summary contains exactly: skill name, source repo, language, scope type, include/exclude patterns, doc sources count, version, forge tier, quality target, description (10 lines), **And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]pprove`, `[E]dit`, `[R]eject`.
16	
17	2. **Given** the user selects `[A]pprove`, **When** validation completes, **Then** the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope is emitted with `mode: "auto"`, **And** the pipeline continues to the health check (and downstream to CS) with the auto-generated brief, **And** no further brief interaction occurs.
18	
19	3. **Given** the user selects `[E]dit`, **When** validation handles the edit, **Then** the user can modify specific fields of the brief (inline natural language adjustments), **And** the modified brief is re-validated against the brief schema via `skf-validate-brief-schema.py`, **And** the modified brief is re-written via the canonical writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py write --from-flat`), **And** the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope is emitted with `mode: "auto"`, **And** the pipeline continues to the health check.
20	
21	4. **Given** the user selects `[R]eject`, **When** validation handles the rejection, **Then** the pipeline does not emit the auto-mode envelope, **And** the brief context variables are hydrated from the auto-generated brief (identical to the ratify hydration in gather-intent §3.1a), **And** `ratify_mode: true` is set in workflow context, **And** the user is informed: "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection", **And** the step chains to `confirm-brief.md` where the full interactive review/edit/revise-scope cycle applies, **And** `write-brief.md` handles the final write and envelope emission (with `mode: null`).
22	
23	5. **Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode, **When** the validation step is reached, **Then** `[A]pprove` is auto-selected (headless default), **And** the pipeline continues without pausing, **And** the auto-action is logged: "headless: auto-approve auto-brief".
24	
25	## Tasks / Subtasks
26	
27	- [ ] Task 1: Modify step-auto-brief.md — defer envelope emission (AC: #2, #3, #4)
28	  - [ ] 1.1 Change frontmatter `nextStepFile` from `'health-check.md'` to `'step-auto-validate.md'`
29	  - [ ] 1.2 Remove §6 (Emit Result Envelope) entirely — envelope emission moves to step-auto-validate.md
30	  - [ ] 1.3 Remove §7 (Error Envelope) canonical section — step-auto-validate inherits the envelope probes and handles both success and error envelopes
31	  - [ ] 1.4 Update §8 heading from "Chain to Health Check" to "Chain to Auto-Validate" and update the description to reference step-auto-validate.md
32	  - [ ] 1.5 Remove `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder` from frontmatter (no longer needed in this step)
33	  - [ ] 1.6 Keep §1-§5 unchanged — the brief MUST be written to disk before the validation step reads it
34	
35	- [ ] Task 2: Create step-auto-validate.md — the auto-brief validation step (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
36	  - [ ] 2.1 Create `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md` with frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'`, `rejectTargetFile: 'confirm-brief.md'`, plus probe orders for `validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder`, `writeSkillBriefProbeOrder`, `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder` (copied from step-auto-brief.md)
37	  - [ ] 2.2 §1 (Load Auto-Brief): read the brief from `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` (written by step-auto-brief §5). HARD HALT if file does not exist (exit code 2, `input-missing`).
38	  - [ ] 2.3 §2 (Present 10-Line Summary): render a concise 10-line summary from the brief fields — skill name, source repo, language, scope type, include/exclude pattern counts, doc sources count, version, forge tier, quality target (90% for deepwiki), description
39	  - [ ] 2.4 §3 (Validation Gate): present `[A]pprove`, `[E]dit`, `[R]eject` menu. Headless gate: auto-select `[A]pprove`, log "headless: auto-approve auto-brief"
40	  - [ ] 2.5 §4 ([A]pprove path): emit `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"` via the emitter script, then chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md)
41	  - [ ] 2.6 §5 ([E]dit path): present editable fields, accept natural language modifications, apply changes, re-validate via `skf-validate-brief-schema.py`, re-assemble flat JSON, re-write via `skf-write-skill-brief.py write --from-flat`, emit envelope with `mode: "auto"`, chain to health-check.md
42	  - [ ] 2.7 §6 ([R]eject path): inform user "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection", hydrate brief context variables from the auto-brief (same field mapping as gather-intent §3.1a ratify hydration), set `ratify_mode: true` and `ratify_source_path`, chain to {rejectTargetFile} (confirm-brief.md)
43	  - [ ] 2.8 §7 (Error Envelope): canonical error envelope pattern for HARD HALTs — emit on stderr via the emitter script with `--target stderr`
44	  - [ ] 2.9 §8 (Chain): load, read fully, then execute the appropriate next step file based on the user's choice
45	
46	- [ ] Task 3: Update BS SKILL.md (AC: #1, #5)
47	  - [ ] 3.1 Add stage 1b to the Stages table: `| 1b | Auto-Brief Validation (auto mode only) | references/step-auto-validate.md | No (interactive gate — headless auto-approves) |`
48	  - [ ] 3.2 Update the paragraph below the Stages table to document the extended auto-mode chain: gather-intent.md §1b → step-auto-brief.md → step-auto-validate.md → health-check.md (on [A]/[E]) or → confirm-brief.md → write-brief.md → health-check.md (on [R])
49	  - [ ] 3.3 No changes to existing interactive stage definitions or Invocation Contract
50	
51	- [ ] Task 4: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #5)
52	  - [ ] 4.1 Verify that the standard `BS` invocation (no `[auto]`) follows the exact same path as before: gather-intent.md → analyze-target.md → scope-definition.md → confirm-brief.md → write-brief.md → health-check.md
53	  - [ ] 4.2 Verify that the `forge` pipeline alias (`BS CS TS EX`) is unaffected (no `[auto]` flag)
54	  - [ ] 4.3 Verify that headless mode (`BS --headless`) without `[auto]` still works correctly
55	  - [ ] 4.4 Verify that the `from_brief` ratify path is unaffected
56	  - [ ] 4.5 Verify that the [R]eject path reuses the existing confirm-brief → write-brief → health-check chain without modifications to those files
57	
58	- [ ] Task 5: Run validation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
59	  - [ ] 5.1 Verify step file frontmatter chain for approve/edit: `step-auto-brief.md` → `step-auto-validate.md` → `health-check.md`
60	  - [ ] 5.2 Verify step file frontmatter chain for reject: `step-auto-validate.md` → `confirm-brief.md` → `write-brief.md` → `health-check.md`
61	  - [ ] 5.3 Verify all references to shared scripts use correct paths with probe-order pattern
62	  - [ ] 5.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes (no regressions)
63	
64	## Dev Notes
65	
66	### Architecture: Validation Step Insertion
67	
68	step-auto-validate.md is inserted between step-auto-brief.md and health-check.md in the auto-mode chain. The key architectural decision is **deferring envelope emission** from step-auto-brief to step-auto-validate:
69	
70	**Before (story 2.2):**
71	```
72	gather-intent §1b → step-auto-brief (write brief + emit envelope) → health-check
73	```
74	
75	**After (story 2.3):**
76	```
77	gather-intent §1b → step-auto-brief (write brief only) → step-auto-validate → health-check
78	```
79	
80	Envelope emission moves to step-auto-validate because:
81	1. On [A]pprove: emit `mode: "auto"` envelope, chain to health-check
82	2. On [E]dit: re-write brief, emit `mode: "auto"` envelope, chain to health-check
83	3. On [R]eject: do NOT emit envelope — the interactive write-brief.md handles its own envelope (with `mode: null`)
84	
85	If the envelope stayed in step-auto-brief, the [R]eject path would produce two envelopes on stdout (one from auto, one from the interactive write-brief), confusing downstream consumers. Moving it to step-auto-validate is the clean separation.
86	
87	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Pipeline & Headless Envelope Patterns — "envelope emitted at the terminal step"]
88	
89	### 10-Line Summary Format
90	
91	The summary gives the user a decision-quality view of the auto-generated brief without the full schema detail. Designed for rapid scanning:
92	
93	```
94	Auto-Brief Summary: {skill_name}
95	─────────────────────────────────
96	Source:       {source_repo}
97	Language:     {language}
98	Scope:        {scope.type} ({N} include, {M} exclude patterns)
99	Docs:         {doc_urls count} sources detected | "None detected"
100	Version:      {version}
101	Forge Tier:   {forge_tier}
102	Quality:      90% target (deepwiki pipeline)
103	Description:  "{description}"
104	```
105	
106	Fields are read directly from the brief on disk (written by step-auto-brief §5). No upstream AN report access required — all summary fields are present in the brief schema.
107	
108	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.3 — "summary contains exactly: shape, scope type, export count, doc sources, quality target (10 lines)"]
109	
110	### [A]pprove Path — Envelope Emission
111	
112	On [A]pprove, emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`:
113	
114	```bash
115	echo '{"status":"success","brief_path":"{brief_path}","skill_name":"{skill_name}","version":"{version}","language":"{language}","scope_type":"{scope_type}","halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}' | \
116	  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit
117	```
118	
119	This is the same envelope that step-auto-brief §6 previously emitted, now deferred to the validation step's approve path.
120	
121	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md#6. Emit Result Envelope]
122	
123	### [E]dit Path — Inline Modification and Re-Write
124	
125	The [E]dit path follows the same inline adjustment pattern as confirm-brief.md §4:
126	
127	1. Present each brief field with current values
128	2. Accept natural language modification requests ("change scope type to public-api", "add doc URL https://...")
129	3. Apply changes to the brief context
130	4. Re-validate the modified context against the brief schema:
131	   ```bash
132	   uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} {brief_path}
133	   ```
134	5. Re-assemble flat JSON (same format as step-auto-brief §4)
135	6. Re-write the brief through the canonical writer:
136	   ```bash
137	   echo '<modified-flat-json>' | uv run {writeSkillBriefHelper} write --target {brief_path} --from-flat
138	   ```
139	7. Emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`
140	8. Chain to health-check.md
141	
142	The edit loop allows multiple modifications — each re-validates before accepting. The user is re-presented the 10-line summary after each modification so they can verify the change. When satisfied, `[A]pprove` proceeds to write and chain.
143	
144	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/confirm-brief.md#4. Handle Inline Adjustments]
145	
146	### [R]eject Path — Interactive Fallback with Pre-Population
147	
148	On [R]eject, the auto pipeline breaks to interactive mode. The step:
149	
150	1. Displays: "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection"
151	2. Hydrates brief context variables from the auto-brief on disk, using the same field mapping as the ratify path in gather-intent §3.1a:
152	   - `name` ← `brief.name`; `version` ← `brief.version`; `target_version` ← `brief.target_version`
153	   - `target_ref` ← `brief.target_ref`; `source_ref` ← `brief.source_ref`
154	   - `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`; `source_type` ← `brief.source_type`; `source_authority` ← `brief.source_authority`; `doc_urls` ← `brief.doc_urls`
155	   - `language` ← `brief.language`; `description` ← `brief.description`; `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
156	   - `created` ← `brief.created`; `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
157	   - `scope.type`/`.include`/`.exclude`/`.tier_a_include`/`.notes`/`.rationale`/`.amendments` ← `brief.scope.*`
158	   - `scripts_intent` ← `brief.scripts_intent`; `assets_intent` ← `brief.assets_intent`
159	3. Sets `ratify_mode: true` and `ratify_source_path: {brief_path}` in workflow context
160	4. Chains to `confirm-brief.md` (not gather-intent or analyze-target — the data is already collected)
161	
162	At confirm-brief.md, the user gets the full review experience:
163	- View the complete brief
164	- Adjust fields inline (§4)
165	- Revise scope boundaries via [R] → scope-definition.md
166	- Approve via [C] → write-brief.md → health-check.md
167	
168	The interactive chain's write-brief.md handles its own envelope emission with `mode: null` (interactive), which is correct since the user explicitly chose to leave auto mode.
169	
170	The brief file written by step-auto-brief will be overwritten by write-brief.md §3 — this is the same overwrite behavior as the ratify path in write-brief.md §2b.
171	
172	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/gather-intent.md#3.1a — ratify hydration field mapping]
173	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/write-brief.md#2b — ratify mode auto-overwrites]
174	
175	### Headless Gate Behavior
176	
177	The validation gate is a **Choice Gate** (default: `[A]pprove`) per headless-gate-convention.md:
178	
179	```
180	**GATE: [A]pprove** — Present [A]pprove / [E]dit / [R]eject to user.
181	If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [A]pprove, log: "headless: auto-approve auto-brief".
182	```
183	
184	In headless mode (which is always true in the deepwiki pipeline), the validation step auto-approves and the pipeline continues without pause. The 10-line summary is still displayed for logging transparency.
185	
186	[Source: src/shared/references/headless-gate-convention.md#Gate Types — "Choice Gate (default: first safe option)"]
187	
188	### Files to Create
189	
190	| File | Purpose |
191	|------|---------|
192	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md` | Auto-brief validation step — load brief → present summary → [A]pprove/[E]dit/[R]eject gate → emit envelope or route to interactive |
193	
194	### Files to Modify
195	
196	| File | Change |
197	|------|--------|
198	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md` | Change nextStepFile to `step-auto-validate.md`; remove §6 (envelope emission), §7 (error envelope), and `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder` from frontmatter; update §8 chain reference |
199	| `src/skf-brief-skill/SKILL.md` | Add stage 1b to Stages table; update auto-mode chain documentation |
200	
201	### Files to Read (Not Modify)
202	
203	| File | Purpose |
204	|------|---------|
205	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/confirm-brief.md` | [R]eject target — understand its §1 ratify-mode handling and §4 inline adjustment pattern |
206	| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/write-brief.md` | Downstream of [R]eject — understand §2b ratify overwrite, §3 flat JSON assembly, §4 envelope emission |
207	| `src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py` | Schema validation for [E]dit re-validation |
208	| `src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py` | Canonical writer for [E]dit re-write |
209	| `src/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py` | Envelope emitter for [A]pprove and [E]dit paths |
210	| `src/shared/references/headless-gate-convention.md` | Choice Gate pattern for headless auto-approve |
211	| `src/skf-brief-skill/assets/skill-brief-schema.md` | Brief schema — field types, constraints, scope object |
212	
213	### Project Structure Notes
214	
215	- step-auto-validate.md goes in `src/skf-brief-skill/references/` alongside existing step files (step-auto-brief.md, gather-intent.md, confirm-brief.md, etc.)
216	- Step naming follows existing pattern: `step-{description}.md` — consistent with story 2.1's `step-auto-scope.md` and story 2.2's `step-auto-brief.md`
217	- No new scripts or Python files — this story creates a markdown step file that invokes existing shared scripts
218	- No new test files — step files are validated structurally, not with pytest
219	
220	### Previous Story Intelligence
221	
222	**Story 2.2 key patterns to replicate:**
223	
224	1. **Envelope emission pattern.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §6 emits the envelope via `skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py emit` with piped JSON. This exact pattern moves to step-auto-validate's [A]pprove and [E]dit paths.
225	
226	2. **Canonical writer invocation.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §5 writes via `skf-write-skill-brief.py write --target <path> --from-flat` with piped flat JSON. The [E]dit path reuses this exact invocation for re-writes.
227	
228	3. **Probe-order resolution.** Story 2.2 uses probe-order arrays for `validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder`, `writeSkillBriefProbeOrder`, and `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder`. step-auto-validate must use the same probe orders for the same scripts.
229	
230	4. **Error envelope on stderr.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §7 emits error envelopes on stderr via `--target stderr`. step-auto-validate's error handling follows the same pattern.
231	
232	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-2-auto-brief-generation-for-bs.md#Dev Notes]
233	
234	**Story 2.2 review fix to learn from:**
235	- Story 2.2's review fixed the envelope emitter to support the `mode` field (schema, Python script, and tests updated). The `mode: "auto"` field is now supported — no additional emitter changes needed for story 2.3.
236	
237	**Story 2.1 pattern to carry forward:**
238	- The `[auto]` routing in init.md and gather-intent.md is a pure conditional branch — zero changes to the interactive path. Story 2.3's validation step follows the same principle: it is a new step inserted into the auto chain only, with zero changes to the interactive chain (confirm-brief.md, write-brief.md unchanged).
239	
240	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-1-auto-scope-mode-for-an.md#Dev Notes]
241	
242	**Epic 1 Retrospective learnings that carry forward:**
243	- Step file insertion pattern is clean and repeatable (stories 1.3, 1.4) — changing one `nextStepFile` frontmatter value. Story 2.3 uses the same pattern: step-auto-brief's `nextStepFile` changes from `health-check.md` to `step-auto-validate.md`.
244	- Contract-driven shared module design — use scripts as-is, delegate deterministic work to them, don't render YAML/JSON in the LLM.
245	
246	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/epic-1-retro-2026-05-26.md]
247	
248	### Git Intelligence
249	
250	Recent commits on `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` branch:
251	- `06652e9` feat(story-2.2): Auto-brief generation for BS
252	- `9d1adc5` feat(story-2.1): Auto-scope mode for AN
253	
254	Stories 2.1 and 2.2 committed sequentially. Story 2.3 continues this pattern.
255	
256	### Git Workflow
257	
258	- **Branch**: `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` (epic-level branch for all Epic 2 stories)
259	- **Commit onto**: This branch, next sequential commit after story 2.2
260	- **PR target**: `main` (single PR when Epic 2 is complete)
261	
262	### Cross-Platform Requirements
263	
264	Per project convention (PRs #311/#316/#366/#368/#387):
265	- Use `encoding="utf-8"` when reading/writing files in any Python code
266	- Use `Path.as_posix()` for paths in JSON output
267	- Windows CI (`windows-latest`) is the gatekeeper — Linux is silent on encoding issues
268	
269	### Dependencies
270	
271	| Dependency | Story | Status | What This Story Needs |
272	|-----------|-------|--------|----------------------|
273	| `step-auto-brief.md` | 2.2 | done | Upstream step that writes the auto-brief to disk; this story modifies its nextStepFile and removes envelope emission |
274	| `confirm-brief.md` | existing | available | [R]eject target — handles ratify-mode hydrated variables for interactive review |
275	| `write-brief.md` | existing | available | Downstream of [R]eject — handles ratify overwrite and envelope emission |
276	| `skf-validate-brief-schema.py` | existing | available | Brief schema validation for [E]dit re-validation |
277	| `skf-write-skill-brief.py` | existing | available | Brief YAML writer for [E]dit re-write |
278	| `skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py` | 2.2 (updated) | available | Envelope emitter with `mode` field support (story 2.2 review fix) |
279	
280	No blocking dependencies. All prerequisite step files and shared modules are complete.
281	
282	### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
283	
284	1. **Do NOT leave envelope emission in step-auto-brief.** The envelope must be deferred to step-auto-validate so that [R]eject does not produce a stale auto-mode envelope followed by a second interactive envelope.
285	
286	2. **Do NOT modify confirm-brief.md or write-brief.md.** The [R]eject path reuses these files as-is. They already handle the ratify-mode case. No changes needed.
287	
288	3. **Do NOT render YAML or JSON envelopes in the LLM.** Delegate to `skf-write-skill-brief.py` and `skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py`, same as step-auto-brief.
289	
290	4. **Do NOT duplicate the ratify hydration field mapping.** The field mapping in the [R]eject path must be identical to gather-intent §3.1a. Reference it, don't reinvent it.
291	
292	5. **Do NOT skip presenting the summary in headless mode.** The summary is displayed for logging transparency even when auto-approving. Only the user interaction (menu choice) is skipped.
293	
294	6. **Do NOT add a separate "quality target" field to the brief.** The 90% quality target comes from the pipeline context (TS[min:90] in the deepwiki alias), not from the brief itself. It is presented for user awareness only.
295

305	- [Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/confirm-brief.md] — [R]eject target: ratify-mode §1, inline adjustment §4, menu §5
306	- [Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/write-brief.md] — downstream of [R]eject: §2b ratify overwrite, §3 flat JSON → writer, §4 envelope emission

# Story 2.3: Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a deepwiki user,
I want to review a 10-line summary of the auto-generated brief before CS runs,
so that I can approve, edit, or reject it — and rejection falls back to interactive BS with pre-populated fields.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** BS auto-brief has been generated, **When** the validation step presents the summary to the user, **Then** the summary contains exactly: skill name, source repo, language, scope type, include/exclude patterns, doc sources count, version, forge tier, quality target, description (10 lines), **And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]pprove`, `[E]dit`, `[R]eject`.

2. **Given** the user selects `[A]pprove`, **When** validation completes, **Then** the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope is emitted with `mode: "auto"`, **And** the pipeline continues to the health check (and downstream to CS) with the auto-generated brief, **And** no further brief interaction occurs.

3. **Given** the user selects `[E]dit`, **When** validation handles the edit, **Then** the user can modify specific fields of the brief (inline natural language adjustments), **And** the modified brief is re-validated against the brief schema via `skf-validate-brief-schema.py`, **And** the modified brief is re-written via the canonical writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py write --from-flat`), **And** the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope is emitted with `mode: "auto"`, **And** the pipeline continues to the health check.

4. **Given** the user selects `[R]eject`, **When** validation handles the rejection, **Then** the pipeline does not emit the auto-mode envelope, **And** the brief context variables are hydrated from the auto-generated brief (identical to the ratify hydration in gather-intent §3.1a), **And** `ratify_mode: true` is set in workflow context, **And** the user is informed: "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection", **And** the step chains to `confirm-brief.md` where the full interactive review/edit/revise-scope cycle applies, **And** `write-brief.md` handles the final write and envelope emission (with `mode: null`).

5. **Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode, **When** the validation step is reached, **Then** `[A]pprove` is auto-selected (headless default), **And** the pipeline continues without pausing, **And** the auto-action is logged: "headless: auto-approve auto-brief".

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Modify step-auto-brief.md — defer envelope emission (AC: #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 1.1 Change frontmatter `nextStepFile` from `'health-check.md'` to `'step-auto-validate.md'`
  - [ ] 1.2 Remove §6 (Emit Result Envelope) entirely — envelope emission moves to step-auto-validate.md
  - [ ] 1.3 Remove §7 (Error Envelope) canonical section — step-auto-validate inherits the envelope probes and handles both success and error envelopes
  - [ ] 1.4 Update §8 heading from "Chain to Health Check" to "Chain to Auto-Validate" and update the description to reference step-auto-validate.md
  - [ ] 1.5 Remove `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder` from frontmatter (no longer needed in this step)
  - [ ] 1.6 Keep §1-§5 unchanged — the brief MUST be written to disk before the validation step reads it

- [ ] Task 2: Create step-auto-validate.md — the auto-brief validation step (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
  - [ ] 2.1 Create `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md` with frontmatter: `nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'`, `rejectTargetFile: 'confirm-brief.md'`, plus probe orders for `validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder`, `writeSkillBriefProbeOrder`, `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder` (copied from step-auto-brief.md)
  - [ ] 2.2 §1 (Load Auto-Brief): read the brief from `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` (written by step-auto-brief §5). HARD HALT if file does not exist (exit code 2, `input-missing`).
  - [ ] 2.3 §2 (Present 10-Line Summary): render a concise 10-line summary from the brief fields — skill name, source repo, language, scope type, include/exclude pattern counts, doc sources count, version, forge tier, quality target (90% for deepwiki), description
  - [ ] 2.4 §3 (Validation Gate): present `[A]pprove`, `[E]dit`, `[R]eject` menu. Headless gate: auto-select `[A]pprove`, log "headless: auto-approve auto-brief"
  - [ ] 2.5 §4 ([A]pprove path): emit `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"` via the emitter script, then chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md)
  - [ ] 2.6 §5 ([E]dit path): present editable fields, accept natural language modifications, apply changes, re-validate via `skf-validate-brief-schema.py`, re-assemble flat JSON, re-write via `skf-write-skill-brief.py write --from-flat`, emit envelope with `mode: "auto"`, chain to health-check.md
  - [ ] 2.7 §6 ([R]eject path): inform user "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection", hydrate brief context variables from the auto-brief (same field mapping as gather-intent §3.1a ratify hydration), set `ratify_mode: true` and `ratify_source_path`, chain to {rejectTargetFile} (confirm-brief.md)
  - [ ] 2.8 §7 (Error Envelope): canonical error envelope pattern for HARD HALTs — emit on stderr via the emitter script with `--target stderr`
  - [ ] 2.9 §8 (Chain): load, read fully, then execute the appropriate next step file based on the user's choice

- [ ] Task 3: Update BS SKILL.md (AC: #1, #5)
  - [ ] 3.1 Add stage 1b to the Stages table: `| 1b | Auto-Brief Validation (auto mode only) | references/step-auto-validate.md | No (interactive gate — headless auto-approves) |`
  - [ ] 3.2 Update the paragraph below the Stages table to document the extended auto-mode chain: gather-intent.md §1b → step-auto-brief.md → step-auto-validate.md → health-check.md (on [A]/[E]) or → confirm-brief.md → write-brief.md → health-check.md (on [R])
  - [ ] 3.3 No changes to existing interactive stage definitions or Invocation Contract

- [ ] Task 4: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #5)
  - [ ] 4.1 Verify that the standard `BS` invocation (no `[auto]`) follows the exact same path as before: gather-intent.md → analyze-target.md → scope-definition.md → confirm-brief.md → write-brief.md → health-check.md
  - [ ] 4.2 Verify that the `forge` pipeline alias (`BS CS TS EX`) is unaffected (no `[auto]` flag)
  - [ ] 4.3 Verify that headless mode (`BS --headless`) without `[auto]` still works correctly
  - [ ] 4.4 Verify that the `from_brief` ratify path is unaffected
  - [ ] 4.5 Verify that the [R]eject path reuses the existing confirm-brief → write-brief → health-check chain without modifications to those files

- [ ] Task 5: Run validation (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4, #5)
  - [ ] 5.1 Verify step file frontmatter chain for approve/edit: `step-auto-brief.md` → `step-auto-validate.md` → `health-check.md`
  - [ ] 5.2 Verify step file frontmatter chain for reject: `step-auto-validate.md` → `confirm-brief.md` → `write-brief.md` → `health-check.md`
  - [ ] 5.3 Verify all references to shared scripts use correct paths with probe-order pattern
  - [ ] 5.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes (no regressions)

## Dev Notes

### Architecture: Validation Step Insertion

step-auto-validate.md is inserted between step-auto-brief.md and health-check.md in the auto-mode chain. The key architectural decision is **deferring envelope emission** from step-auto-brief to step-auto-validate:

**Before (story 2.2):**
```
gather-intent §1b → step-auto-brief (write brief + emit envelope) → health-check
```

**After (story 2.3):**
```
gather-intent §1b → step-auto-brief (write brief only) → step-auto-validate → health-check
```

Envelope emission moves to step-auto-validate because:
1. On [A]pprove: emit `mode: "auto"` envelope, chain to health-check
2. On [E]dit: re-write brief, emit `mode: "auto"` envelope, chain to health-check
3. On [R]eject: do NOT emit envelope — the interactive write-brief.md handles its own envelope (with `mode: null`)

If the envelope stayed in step-auto-brief, the [R]eject path would produce two envelopes on stdout (one from auto, one from the interactive write-brief), confusing downstream consumers. Moving it to step-auto-validate is the clean separation.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Pipeline & Headless Envelope Patterns — "envelope emitted at the terminal step"]

### 10-Line Summary Format

The summary gives the user a decision-quality view of the auto-generated brief without the full schema detail. Designed for rapid scanning:

```
Auto-Brief Summary: {skill_name}
─────────────────────────────────
Source:       {source_repo}
Language:     {language}
Scope:        {scope.type} ({N} include, {M} exclude patterns)
Docs:         {doc_urls count} sources detected | "None detected"
Version:      {version}
Forge Tier:   {forge_tier}
Quality:      90% target (deepwiki pipeline)
Description:  "{description}"
```

Fields are read directly from the brief on disk (written by step-auto-brief §5). No upstream AN report access required — all summary fields are present in the brief schema.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.3 — "summary contains exactly: shape, scope type, export count, doc sources, quality target (10 lines)"]

### [A]pprove Path — Envelope Emission

On [A]pprove, emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`:

```bash
echo '{"status":"success","brief_path":"{brief_path}","skill_name":"{skill_name}","version":"{version}","language":"{language}","scope_type":"{scope_type}","halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}' | \
  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit
```

This is the same envelope that step-auto-brief §6 previously emitted, now deferred to the validation step's approve path.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md#6. Emit Result Envelope]

### [E]dit Path — Inline Modification and Re-Write

The [E]dit path follows the same inline adjustment pattern as confirm-brief.md §4:

1. Present each brief field with current values
2. Accept natural language modification requests ("change scope type to public-api", "add doc URL https://...")
3. Apply changes to the brief context
4. Re-validate the modified context against the brief schema:
   ```bash
   uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} {brief_path}
   ```
5. Re-assemble flat JSON (same format as step-auto-brief §4)
6. Re-write the brief through the canonical writer:
   ```bash
   echo '<modified-flat-json>' | uv run {writeSkillBriefHelper} write --target {brief_path} --from-flat
   ```
7. Emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`
8. Chain to health-check.md

The edit loop allows multiple modifications — each re-validates before accepting. The user is re-presented the 10-line summary after each modification so they can verify the change. When satisfied, `[A]pprove` proceeds to write and chain.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/confirm-brief.md#4. Handle Inline Adjustments]

### [R]eject Path — Interactive Fallback with Pre-Population

On [R]eject, the auto pipeline breaks to interactive mode. The step:

1. Displays: "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection"
2. Hydrates brief context variables from the auto-brief on disk, using the same field mapping as the ratify path in gather-intent §3.1a:
   - `name` ← `brief.name`; `version` ← `brief.version`; `target_version` ← `brief.target_version`
   - `target_ref` ← `brief.target_ref`; `source_ref` ← `brief.source_ref`
   - `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`; `source_type` ← `brief.source_type`; `source_authority` ← `brief.source_authority`; `doc_urls` ← `brief.doc_urls`
   - `language` ← `brief.language`; `description` ← `brief.description`; `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
   - `created` ← `brief.created`; `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
   - `scope.type`/`.include`/`.exclude`/`.tier_a_include`/`.notes`/`.rationale`/`.amendments` ← `brief.scope.*`
   - `scripts_intent` ← `brief.scripts_intent`; `assets_intent` ← `brief.assets_intent`
3. Sets `ratify_mode: true` and `ratify_source_path: {brief_path}` in workflow context
4. Chains to `confirm-brief.md` (not gather-intent or analyze-target — the data is already collected)

At confirm-brief.md, the user gets the full review experience:
- View the complete brief
- Adjust fields inline (§4)
- Revise scope boundaries via [R] → scope-definition.md
- Approve via [C] → write-brief.md → health-check.md

The interactive chain's write-brief.md handles its own envelope emission with `mode: null` (interactive), which is correct since the user explicitly chose to leave auto mode.

The brief file written by step-auto-brief will be overwritten by write-brief.md §3 — this is the same overwrite behavior as the ratify path in write-brief.md §2b.

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/gather-intent.md#3.1a — ratify hydration field mapping]
[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/write-brief.md#2b — ratify mode auto-overwrites]

### Headless Gate Behavior

The validation gate is a **Choice Gate** (default: `[A]pprove`) per headless-gate-convention.md:

```
**GATE: [A]pprove** — Present [A]pprove / [E]dit / [R]eject to user.
If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [A]pprove, log: "headless: auto-approve auto-brief".
```

In headless mode (which is always true in the deepwiki pipeline), the validation step auto-approves and the pipeline continues without pause. The 10-line summary is still displayed for logging transparency.

[Source: src/shared/references/headless-gate-convention.md#Gate Types — "Choice Gate (default: first safe option)"]

### Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md` | Auto-brief validation step — load brief → present summary → [A]pprove/[E]dit/[R]eject gate → emit envelope or route to interactive |

### Project Structure Notes

- step-auto-validate.md goes in `src/skf-brief-skill/references/` alongside existing step files (step-auto-brief.md, gather-intent.md, confirm-brief.md, etc.)
- Step naming follows existing pattern: `step-{description}.md` — consistent with story 2.1's `step-auto-scope.md` and story 2.2's `step-auto-brief.md`

- No new test files — step files are validated structurally, not with pytest

### Previous Story Intelligence

**Story 2.2 key patterns to replicate:**

1. **Envelope emission pattern.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §6 emits the envelope via `skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py emit` with piped JSON. This exact pattern moves to step-auto-validate's [A]pprove and [E]dit paths.

2. **Canonical writer invocation.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §5 writes via `skf-write-skill-brief.py write --target <path> --from-flat` with piped flat JSON. The [E]dit path reuses this exact invocation for re-writes.

3. **Probe-order resolution.** Story 2.2 uses probe-order arrays for `validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder`, `writeSkillBriefProbeOrder`, and `emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder`. step-auto-validate must use the same probe orders for the same scripts.

4. **Error envelope on stderr.** Story 2.2's step-auto-brief §7 emits error envelopes on stderr via `--target stderr`. step-auto-validate's error handling follows the same pattern.

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-2-auto-brief-generation-for-bs.md#Dev Notes]

**Story 2.2 review fix to learn from:**

**Story 2.1 pattern to carry forward:**
- The `[auto]` routing in init.md and gather-intent.md is a pure conditional branch — zero changes to the interactive path. Story 2.3's validation step follows the same principle: it is a new step inserted into the auto chain only, with zero changes to the interactive chain (confirm-brief.md, write-brief.md unchanged).

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-1-auto-scope-mode-for-an.md#Dev Notes]

**Epic 1 Retrospective learnings that carry forward:**
- Step file insertion pattern is clean and repeatable (stories 1.3, 1.4) — changing one `nextStepFile` frontmatter value. Story 2.3 uses the same pattern: step-auto-brief's `nextStepFile` changes from `health-check.md` to `step-auto-validate.md`.
- Contract-driven shared module design — use scripts as-is, delegate deterministic work to them, don't render YAML/JSON in the LLM.

[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/epic-1-retro-2026-05-26.md]

### Dependencies

|-----------|-------|--------|----------------------|

No blocking dependencies. All prerequisite step files and shared modules are complete.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

1. **Do NOT leave envelope emission in step-auto-brief.** The envelope must be deferred to step-auto-validate so that [R]eject does not produce a stale auto-mode envelope followed by a second interactive envelope.

2. **Do NOT modify confirm-brief.md or write-brief.md.** The [R]eject path reuses these files as-is. They already handle the ratify-mode case. No changes needed.

3. **Do NOT render YAML or JSON envelopes in the LLM.** Delegate to `skf-write-skill-brief.py` and `skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py`, same as step-auto-brief.

4. **Do NOT duplicate the ratify hydration field mapping.** The field mapping in the [R]eject path must be identical to gather-intent §3.1a. Reference it, don't reinvent it.

5. **Do NOT skip presenting the summary in headless mode.** The summary is displayed for logging transparency even when auto-approving. Only the user interaction (menu choice) is skipped.

6. **Do NOT add a separate "quality target" field to the brief.** The 90% quality target comes from the pipeline context (TS[min:90] in the deepwiki alias), not from the brief itself. It is presented for user awareness only.

1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'

11	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
12	detectDocsScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py'
13	---
14	
15	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
16	
17	# Step 1a: Auto-Brief Generation
18	
19	## STEP GOAL:
20	
21	To enrich an upstream skill brief (produced by AN auto-scope) with documentation URLs discovered via `skf-detect-docs.py`, validate the enriched brief, write it through the canonical writer, and emit the headless result envelope. This step replaces the interactive gather-intent → analyze-target → scope-definition → confirm-brief → write-brief chain when `[auto]` mode is active.
22	
23	## Rules
24	
25	- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
26	- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
27	- Must produce the same output artifact as the interactive chain: a validated `skill-brief.yaml`
28	- Doc detection is best-effort — failures do not halt the pipeline
29	- Do NOT re-derive scope fields from the upstream brief — AN already set them correctly
30	- Do NOT render YAML or JSON envelopes in the LLM — delegate to deterministic scripts
31	
32	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
33	
34	### 1. Load Upstream Brief
35	
36	Read the upstream brief path from `{brief_path}` (passed by the forger from AN's `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` `brief_paths[]`).
37	
38	**IF `{brief_path}` is not set or the file does not exist:**
39	- HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-missing`): "**Auto-brief requires an upstream brief — `brief_path` is missing or the file does not exist at `{brief_path}`.**"
40	- Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-missing"`.
41	
42	**Resolve `{validateBriefSchemaHelper}`** from `{validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
43	
44	Validate the upstream brief against the schema:
45	
46	```bash
47	uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} {brief_path}
48	```
49	
50	The script returns JSON `{valid, errors[], warnings[], halt_reason, brief}`.
51	
52	- **`valid: false`** — the upstream brief is malformed. HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-invalid`): "**Upstream brief at `{brief_path}` is invalid: {first error message}.**" Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`.
53	- **`valid: true`** — proceed with the parsed `brief` payload. Surface any non-empty `warnings[]` to the log.
54	
55	Extract from the parsed brief:
56	- `skill_name` ← `brief.name`
57	- `version` ← `brief.version`
58	- `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`
59	- `language` ← `brief.language`
60	- `scope_type` ← `brief.scope.type`
61	- `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
62	- `description` ← `brief.description`
63	- `created` ← `brief.created`
64	- `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
65	- All scope fields: `scope.include`, `scope.exclude`, `scope.notes`, `scope.rationale`, `scope.amendments`, `scope.tier_a_include`
66	- Optional fields: `source_type`, `source_authority`, `doc_urls`, `target_version`, `target_ref`, `source_ref`, `scripts_intent`, `assets_intent`
67	
68	### 2. Run Doc Detection
69	
70	Invoke doc detection to discover documentation URLs for the source repo:
71	
72	```bash
73	uv run python {project-root}/{detectDocsScript} --repo-url {source_repo}
74	```
75	
76	`--repo-url` is always required (the script uses it for GitHub API calls). If a local clone is also available at `{local_clone_path}`, add `--local-path {local_clone_path}` to enable docs-folder scanning in addition to API-based detection.
77	
78	**Handle exit codes:**
79	
80	- **Exit 0 (found docs):** Parse the JSON output array. Each entry has `{url, detected_via, content_hash, content_type}`. Proceed to §3 with the detected docs.
81	- **Exit 1 (none found):** Log: "No external documentation found — brief generated from source analysis only." Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.
82	- **Exit 2 (error):** Log warning: "Doc detection failed — proceeding without doc enrichment." Do NOT halt — doc enrichment is best-effort. Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.
83	
84	### 3. Enrich Brief with Detected Docs
85	
86	For each detected doc entry, create a brief `doc_urls` entry:
87	
88	- `url` ← `url` (direct copy)
89	- `label` ← derive from `content_type` if available:
90	  - `"api-docs"` → `"API Documentation"`
91	  - `"guide"` → `"Guide"`
92	  - `"reference"` → `"Reference"`
93	  - Otherwise derive from `detected_via`:
94	    - `"homepageUrl"` → `"Homepage"`
95	    - `"readme_link"` → `"README Link"`
96	    - `"pages_api"` → `"GitHub Pages"`
97	    - `"docs_folder"` → `"Docs Folder"`
98	
99	If the upstream brief already has `doc_urls`, merge the detected docs with the existing entries (deduplicate by URL — existing entries take precedence).
100	
101	### 4. Validate Enriched Brief
102	
103	Assemble the enriched brief context as a flat JSON object following the write-brief §3 contract:
104	
105	```json
106	{
107	  "name":             "{skill_name}",
108	  "target_version":   "{target_version or null}",
109	  "detected_version": null,
110	  "source_type":      "{source_type or 'source'}",
111	  "source_repo":      "{source_repo}",
112	  "language":         "{language}",
113	  "description":      "{description}",
114	  "forge_tier":       "{forge_tier}",
115	  "created":          "{created}",
116	  "created_by":       "{created_by}",
117	  "scope_type":       "{scope_type}",
118	  "scope_include":    ["{scope.include patterns}"],
119	  "scope_exclude":    ["{scope.exclude patterns}"],
120	  "scope_notes":      "{scope.notes or ''}",
121	  "scope_rationale":  null,
122	  "scope_tier_a_include": null,
123	  "scope_amendments":     null,
124	  "doc_urls":         [{"url": "...", "label": "..."}],
125	  "scripts_intent":   "{scripts_intent or null}",
126	  "assets_intent":    "{assets_intent or null}",
127	  "source_authority": "{source_authority or null}",
128	  "target_ref":       "{target_ref or null}",
129	  "source_ref":       "{source_ref or null}",
130	  "version_resolved": "{version}"
131	}
132	```
133	
134	The `version_resolved` key pins the output to the upstream brief's version — without it, the writer's precedence logic falls through to `1.0.0` since `target_version` and `detected_version` are both null on the auto path.
135	
136	### 5. Write Enriched Brief
137	
138	**Resolve `{writeSkillBriefHelper}`** from `{writeSkillBriefProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
139	
140	Write the enriched brief through the canonical writer:
141	
142	```bash
143	echo '<context-json>' | uv run {writeSkillBriefHelper} write --target {forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml --from-flat
144	```
145	
146	**On script failure (non-zero exit):**
147	- Exit 1 (validation/invariant): Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`, then HARD HALT.
148	- Exit 2 (I/O failure): Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "write-failed"`, then HARD HALT.
149	
150	**On success:** Capture `brief_path` and `version` from the response envelope for §6.
151	
152	### 6. Emit Result Envelope
153	
154	**Resolve `{emitBriefEnvelopeHelper}`** from `{emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.
155	
156	Emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`:
157	
158	```bash
159	echo '{"status":"success","brief_path":"{brief_path from §5}","skill_name":"{skill_name}","version":"{version from §5}","language":"{language}","scope_type":"{scope_type}","halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}' | \
160	  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit
161	```
162	
163	### 7. Error Envelope (Canonical)
164	
165	Every HARD HALT in this step emits the error envelope on stderr:
166	
167	```bash
168	echo '{"status":"error","skill_name":"{skill_name or unknown}","halt_reason":"{reason}","mode":"auto"}' | \
169	  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit --target stderr
170	```
171	
172	### 8. Chain to Health Check
173	
174	Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.
175	
176	## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
177	
178	ONLY WHEN the enriched brief has been written, validated, and the result envelope emitted will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.

---
nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'
validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder:
  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
writeSkillBriefProbeOrder:
  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py'
  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-write-skill-brief.py'
emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder:
  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
detectDocsScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py'
---

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 1a: Auto-Brief Generation

## STEP GOAL:

To enrich an upstream skill brief (produced by AN auto-scope) with documentation URLs discovered via `skf-detect-docs.py`, validate the enriched brief, write it through the canonical writer, and emit the headless result envelope. This step replaces the interactive gather-intent → analyze-target → scope-definition → confirm-brief → write-brief chain when `[auto]` mode is active.

## Rules

- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
- Must produce the same output artifact as the interactive chain: a validated `skill-brief.yaml`
- Doc detection is best-effort — failures do not halt the pipeline
- Do NOT re-derive scope fields from the upstream brief — AN already set them correctly
- Do NOT render YAML or JSON envelopes in the LLM — delegate to deterministic scripts

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Load Upstream Brief

Read the upstream brief path from `{brief_path}` (passed by the forger from AN's `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` `brief_paths[]`).

**IF `{brief_path}` is not set or the file does not exist:**
- HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-missing`): "**Auto-brief requires an upstream brief — `brief_path` is missing or the file does not exist at `{brief_path}`.**"
- Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-missing"`.

**Resolve `{validateBriefSchemaHelper}`** from `{validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.

Validate the upstream brief against the schema:

```bash
uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} {brief_path}
```

The script returns JSON `{valid, errors[], warnings[], halt_reason, brief}`.

- **`valid: false`** — the upstream brief is malformed. HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-invalid`): "**Upstream brief at `{brief_path}` is invalid: {first error message}.**" Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`.
- **`valid: true`** — proceed with the parsed `brief` payload. Surface any non-empty `warnings[]` to the log.

Extract from the parsed brief:
- `skill_name` ← `brief.name`
- `version` ← `brief.version`
- `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`
- `language` ← `brief.language`
- `scope_type` ← `brief.scope.type`
- `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
- `description` ← `brief.description`
- `created` ← `brief.created`
- `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
- All scope fields: `scope.include`, `scope.exclude`, `scope.notes`, `scope.rationale`, `scope.amendments`, `scope.tier_a_include`
- Optional fields: `source_type`, `source_authority`, `doc_urls`, `target_version`, `target_ref`, `source_ref`, `scripts_intent`, `assets_intent`

### 2. Run Doc Detection

Invoke doc detection to discover documentation URLs for the source repo:

```bash
uv run python {project-root}/{detectDocsScript} --repo-url {source_repo}
```

`--repo-url` is always required (the script uses it for GitHub API calls). If a local clone is also available at `{local_clone_path}`, add `--local-path {local_clone_path}` to enable docs-folder scanning in addition to API-based detection.

**Handle exit codes:**

- **Exit 0 (found docs):** Parse the JSON output array. Each entry has `{url, detected_via, content_hash, content_type}`. Proceed to §3 with the detected docs.
- **Exit 1 (none found):** Log: "No external documentation found — brief generated from source analysis only." Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.
- **Exit 2 (error):** Log warning: "Doc detection failed — proceeding without doc enrichment." Do NOT halt — doc enrichment is best-effort. Proceed to §4 with no doc enrichment.

### 3. Enrich Brief with Detected Docs

For each detected doc entry, create a brief `doc_urls` entry:

- `url` ← `url` (direct copy)
- `label` ← derive from `content_type` if available:
  - `"api-docs"` → `"API Documentation"`
  - `"guide"` → `"Guide"`
  - `"reference"` → `"Reference"`
  - Otherwise derive from `detected_via`:
    - `"homepageUrl"` → `"Homepage"`
    - `"readme_link"` → `"README Link"`
    - `"pages_api"` → `"GitHub Pages"`
    - `"docs_folder"` → `"Docs Folder"`

If the upstream brief already has `doc_urls`, merge the detected docs with the existing entries (deduplicate by URL — existing entries take precedence).

### 4. Validate Enriched Brief

Assemble the enriched brief context as a flat JSON object following the write-brief §3 contract:

```json
{
  "name":             "{skill_name}",
  "target_version":   "{target_version or null}",
  "detected_version": null,
  "source_type":      "{source_type or 'source'}",
  "source_repo":      "{source_repo}",
  "language":         "{language}",
  "description":      "{description}",
  "forge_tier":       "{forge_tier}",
  "created":          "{created}",
  "created_by":       "{created_by}",
  "scope_type":       "{scope_type}",
  "scope_include":    ["{scope.include patterns}"],
  "scope_exclude":    ["{scope.exclude patterns}"],
  "scope_notes":      "{scope.notes or ''}",
  "scope_rationale":  null,
  "scope_tier_a_include": null,
  "scope_amendments":     null,
  "doc_urls":         [{"url": "...", "label": "..."}],
  "scripts_intent":   "{scripts_intent or null}",
  "assets_intent":    "{assets_intent or null}",
  "source_authority": "{source_authority or null}",
  "target_ref":       "{target_ref or null}",
  "source_ref":       "{source_ref or null}",
  "version_resolved": "{version}"
}
```

The `version_resolved` key pins the output to the upstream brief's version — without it, the writer's precedence logic falls through to `1.0.0` since `target_version` and `detected_version` are both null on the auto path.

### 5. Write Enriched Brief

**Resolve `{writeSkillBriefHelper}`** from `{writeSkillBriefProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.

Write the enriched brief through the canonical writer:

```bash
echo '<context-json>' | uv run {writeSkillBriefHelper} write --target {forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml --from-flat
```

**On script failure (non-zero exit):**
- Exit 1 (validation/invariant): Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "input-invalid"`, then HARD HALT.
- Exit 2 (I/O failure): Emit error envelope per §7 with `halt_reason: "write-failed"`, then HARD HALT.

**On success:** Capture `brief_path` and `version` from the response envelope for §6.

### 6. Emit Result Envelope

**Resolve `{emitBriefEnvelopeHelper}`** from `{emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder}`; first existing path wins. HALT if no candidate exists.

Emit the `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` envelope with `mode: "auto"`:

```bash
echo '{"status":"success","brief_path":"{brief_path from §5}","skill_name":"{skill_name}","version":"{version from §5}","language":"{language}","scope_type":"{scope_type}","halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}' | \
  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit
```

### 7. Error Envelope (Canonical)

Every HARD HALT in this step emits the error envelope on stderr:

```bash
echo '{"status":"error","skill_name":"{skill_name or unknown}","halt_reason":"{reason}","mode":"auto"}' | \
  uv run {emitBriefEnvelopeHelper} emit --target stderr
```

### 8. Chain to Health Check

Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN the enriched brief has been written, validated, and the result envelope emitted will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: skf-brief-skill
3	description: Design a skill scope through guided discovery. Use when the user requests to "create a skill brief" or "brief a skill".
4	---
5	
6	# Brief Skill
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	Helps the user define what to skill — target repo, scope, language, inclusion/exclusion patterns — and produces a `skill-brief.yaml` that drives create-skill. This is the first step in the skill creation pipeline; the brief is the input contract for create-skill, which performs the actual compilation.
11	
12	A good skill brief sets a tight, cohesive boundary: one capability with 3-8 primary functions, an unambiguous public API surface, and a description short enough to fit in a registry row. Briefs that try to cover several unrelated concerns (e.g. authentication *and* data visualization) compile into skills that no agent can route to confidently — a brief covering too much is a worse failure mode than a brief covering too little, and this workflow steers toward the smaller, sharper version when scope is unclear.
13	
14	Brief-skill is split from create-skill so the scoping conversation runs *once*, on cheap signals (manifests, top-level exports, intent), without paying for AST extraction. Compilation is expensive; scoping decisions are cheap to revise. Keeping them in separate workflows lets a user iterate on the brief, share it for review, and re-run create-skill against the same brief whenever the upstream version moves.
15	
16	**Ratify path.** When the user already has a `skill-brief.yaml` produced by another workflow — typically `skf-analyze-source`'s `generate-briefs` step, where one analyze pass emits several recommended briefs — the brief can be *ratified* (reviewed and rewritten without re-deriving its fields) instead of authored from scratch. **Interactively:** invoke `/skf-brief-skill` with a path to that brief at the first prompt — gather-intent §3.1a loads the YAML, hydrates the brief context, and jumps straight to step 4 (confirm-brief) where the standard review/edit cycle still applies before step 5 writes. **Headlessly:** pass `from_brief <path>` — the step 1 §8 GATE runs the same schema validation and hydration and writes through the canonical writer (overwriting in place), enabling a fully headless analyze → brief → create pipeline that preserves the upstream-authored scope. Either path skips re-deriving fields the upstream draft already supplies and saves the 5-10 minutes a full re-run of intent + analyze + scope would cost.
17	
18	## Conventions
19	
20	- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
21	- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
22	- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
23	- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
24	- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.
25	
26	## Role
27	
28	You are a skill scoping architect collaborating with a developer who wants to create an agent skill. You bring expertise in source code analysis, API surface identification, and skill boundary design, while the user brings their domain knowledge and specific use case. Work together as equals.
29	
30	## Workflow Rules
31	
32	These rules apply to every step in this workflow:
33	
34	- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
35	- **Lazy-load references and assets:** `references/*.md` and `assets/*.md` files are loaded inside the section that needs them, not at step entry. If a section is skipped (e.g. `version-resolution.md` when `{extractPublicApiHelper}` already returned a version, `scope-templates.md` for the `docs-only` branch that bypasses §2c), do not load that file. Each unnecessary load costs context (~5-10 KB per reference) and biases the LLM toward consulting material the current path does not need.
36	- Always communicate in `{communication_language}` (the language for user-facing prose). Written artifact text — the `description`, `notes`, and other free-form fields persisted into `skill-brief.yaml` — is in `{document_output_language}`; per-step rules call this out where it applies (see step 5). The two values may be the same.
37	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
38	
39	## On Activation
40	
41	1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
42	   - `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`
43	
44	2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.
45	
46	3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:
47	
48	   ```bash
49	   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
50	       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
51	   ```
52	
53	   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):
54	
55	   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
56	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
57	   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)
58	
59	   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.
60	
61	   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each of the three scalars, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:
62	
63	   - `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` ← `workflow.description_voice_examples_path` if non-empty, else `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
64	   - `{scopeTemplatesPath}` ← `workflow.scope_templates_path` if non-empty, else `assets/scope-templates.md`
65	   - `{briefSchemaPath}` ← `workflow.brief_schema_path` if non-empty, else `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`
66	
67	   Stash all three as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` / `{scopeTemplatesPath}` / `{briefSchemaPath}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies without forking the skill.
68	
69	4. Load, read the full file, and execute `references/gather-intent.md`.
70	
71	## Stages
72	
73	| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
74	|---|------|------|--------------|
75	| 1 | Gather Intent | references/gather-intent.md | No (interactive) |
76	| 1a | Auto-Brief Generation (auto mode only) | references/step-auto-brief.md | Yes |
77	| 2 | Analyze Target | references/analyze-target.md | Yes |
78	| 3 | Scope Definition | references/scope-definition.md | No (interactive) |
79	| 4 | Confirm Brief | references/confirm-brief.md | No (confirm) |
80	| 5 | Write Brief | references/write-brief.md | Yes |
81	| 6 | Workflow Health Check (terminal) | references/health-check.md | Yes |
82	
83	Stage 1a is conditional — it replaces stages 2-5 when BS is invoked with the `[auto]` flag via pipeline context. The routing decision is made in stage 1 (gather-intent.md §1b). In auto mode, the chain is: gather-intent.md §1 (forge tier) → §1b (auto check) → step-auto-brief.md → health-check.md.
84	
85	## Invocation Contract
86	
87	| Aspect | Detail |
88	|--------|--------|
89	| **Inputs** | `target_repo` [required], `skill_name` [required], `scope_hint` [optional], `language_hint` [optional], `target_version` [optional], `source_authority` [optional: official/community/internal, default community], `source_type` [optional: source/docs-only, default source], `doc_urls` [optional: list of `url[,label]` for source_type=docs-only or supplemental], `scope_type` [optional: full-library/specific-modules/public-api/component-library/reference-app/docs-only], `include` [optional: comma-separated globs], `exclude` [optional: comma-separated globs], `scripts_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `assets_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `intent` [optional: free-text used to derive description], `force` [optional: overwrite existing brief without prompting], `from_brief` [optional: path to a pre-authored `skill-brief.yaml` to *ratify* — when supplied it is the source of truth, `target_repo`/`skill_name` become optional/derived-from-brief, and the run mirrors the interactive §3.1a ratify path: schema-validate, skip analyze-target/scope-definition, write through the canonical writer in place], `[auto]` [optional: bracket modifier passed via pipeline context — when present, BS loads the upstream brief from `brief_path` in pipeline data, enriches it with doc detection, and writes through the canonical writer; requires `brief_path` from AN's `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`] |
90	| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args] | step 3: Confirm Gate [C] | step 4: Confirm Gate [C] |
91	| **Outputs** | `skill-brief.yaml` at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`; final `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` line on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true |
92	| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with heuristic-driven or default action when `{headless_mode}` is true; pre-supplied inputs consumed at the gates that would otherwise prompt; absent `source_authority` and `scope_type` are resolved by signal-driven detection (see `references/headless-args.md`); existing briefs are preserved unless `--force` was supplied (HALT with `overwrite-cancelled` otherwise); supplying `from_brief <path>` instead routes the step 1 GATE to a ratify path that schema-validates the pre-authored brief, skips analyze-target/scope-definition, and writes through the canonical writer (overwriting in place, no `--force` needed) rather than deriving a new brief |
93	| **Transient-failure retry** | This workflow does **not** auto-retry network or subprocess failures. A failed `gh` fetch (analyze-target.md §1, portfolio-similarity-check.md), QMD probe, or extraction script is logged and surfaced in the final result envelope as a warning, but the workflow continues with whatever signal it has. Headless pipelines that want retry semantics should wrap the invocation at their orchestrator level (e.g. CI re-runner on non-zero exit). Rationale: brief-skill is read-mostly with one terminal write (the YAML at step 5); a partial-signal retry has more failure modes than just re-running the whole workflow, which is cheap. |
94	| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |
95	
96	## Exit Codes
97	
98	Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:
99	
100	| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
101	| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
102	| 0    | success              | step 6 (terminal)                                                                         |
103	| 2    | input-missing / input-invalid | step 1 GATE — required headless arg absent (`target_repo`, `skill_name`, or `doc_urls` when `source_type=docs-only`) → `input-missing`; enum violation, malformed semver, non-kebab `skill_name`, or step 5 brief-context schema validation failure → `input-invalid` |
104	| 3    | resolution-failure   | step 1 §1 (`forge-tier.yaml` missing); step 2 §1 (target inaccessible / `gh auth` fails) |
105	| 4    | write-failure        | step 1 §1 pre-flight write probe (data folder unwritable: read-only mount, disk full, permissions denied); step 5 §4 (write to `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml` failed) |
106	| 5    | overwrite-cancelled  | step 5 §2 (existing brief, `force` not supplied)                                          |
107	| 6    | user-cancelled       | any interactive menu in step 1/03/04 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)                |
108	
109	## Result Contract (Headless)
110	
111	When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 5 emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 6, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:
112	
113	```
114	SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","brief_path":"…|null","skill_name":"…","version":"…|null","language":"…|null","scope_type":"…|null","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto|null"}
115	```
116	
117	`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path, `"error"` on any HALT. `halt_reason` is one of: `null` (success), `"input-missing"`, `"input-invalid"`, `"forge-tier-missing"`, `"target-inaccessible"`, `"gh-auth-failed"`, `"write-failed"`, `"overwrite-cancelled"`, `"user-cancelled"`. `exit_code` matches the table above. `mode` is `"auto"` when BS was invoked with the `[auto]` flag (pipeline auto-brief generation), `null` otherwise (interactive or headless-without-auto).
118

## Conventions

- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).

- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

## Workflow Rules

These rules apply to every step in this workflow:

- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
- **Lazy-load references and assets:** `references/*.md` and `assets/*.md` files are loaded inside the section that needs them, not at step entry. If a section is skipped (e.g. `version-resolution.md` when `{extractPublicApiHelper}` already returned a version, `scope-templates.md` for the `docs-only` branch that bypasses §2c), do not load that file. Each unnecessary load costs context (~5-10 KB per reference) and biases the LLM toward consulting material the current path does not need.

The two values may be the same.

## On Activation

1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
   - `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`

2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.

3. **Resolve workflow customization.** Run:

   ```bash
   python3 {project-root}/_bmad/scripts/resolve_customization.py \
       --skill {skill-root} --key workflow
   ```

   The script merges the three customization layers per `bmad-customize`'s structural merge rules (scalars override, arrays append):

   - `{skill-root}/customize.toml` — bundled defaults
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.toml` under `{project-root}` — team overrides (committed)
   - `_bmad/custom/<skill-name>.user.toml` under `{project-root}` — personal overrides (gitignored)

   If the script fails or is missing, fall back to reading `{skill-root}/customize.toml` directly — the bundled defaults are an empty string for each path scalar.

   Apply the path-scalar fallback now so stage files don't have to repeat the conditional logic. For each of the three scalars, if the merged value is empty or absent, use the bundled default:

   - `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` ← `workflow.description_voice_examples_path` if non-empty, else `assets/description-voice-examples.md`
   - `{scopeTemplatesPath}` ← `workflow.scope_templates_path` if non-empty, else `assets/scope-templates.md`
   - `{briefSchemaPath}` ← `workflow.brief_schema_path` if non-empty, else `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`

   Stash all three as workflow-context variables. Stage files reference `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` / `{scopeTemplatesPath}` / `{briefSchemaPath}` directly — no conditional at the usage site. Empty-string overrides cleanly fall through to the bundled default; non-empty values let orgs swap in house-style copies without forking the skill.

4. Load, read the full file, and execute `references/gather-intent.md`.

## Stages

| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
|---|------|------|--------------|
| 1 | Gather Intent | references/gather-intent.md | No (interactive) |
| 1a | Auto-Brief Generation (auto mode only) | references/step-auto-brief.md | Yes |
| 2 | Analyze Target | references/analyze-target.md | Yes |
| 3 | Scope Definition | references/scope-definition.md | No (interactive) |
| 4 | Confirm Brief | references/confirm-brief.md | No (confirm) |
| 5 | Write Brief | references/write-brief.md | Yes |
| 6 | Workflow Health Check (terminal) | references/health-check.md | Yes |

Stage 1a is conditional — it replaces stages 2-5 when BS is invoked with the `[auto]` flag via pipeline context. The routing decision is made in stage 1 (gather-intent.md §1b). In auto mode, the chain is: gather-intent.md §1 (forge tier) → §1b (auto check) → step-auto-brief.md → health-check.md.

## Invocation Contract

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| **Inputs** | `target_repo` [required], `skill_name` [required], `scope_hint` [optional], `language_hint` [optional], `target_version` [optional], `source_authority` [optional: official/community/internal, default community], `source_type` [optional: source/docs-only, default source], `doc_urls` [optional: list of `url[,label]` for source_type=docs-only or supplemental], `scope_type` [optional: full-library/specific-modules/public-api/component-library/reference-app/docs-only], `include` [optional: comma-separated globs], `exclude` [optional: comma-separated globs], `scripts_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `assets_intent` [optional: detect/none/free-text, default detect], `intent` [optional: free-text used to derive description], `force` [optional: overwrite existing brief without prompting], `from_brief` [optional: path to a pre-authored `skill-brief.yaml` to *ratify* — when supplied it is the source of truth, `target_repo`/`skill_name` become optional/derived-from-brief, and the run mirrors the interactive §3.1a ratify path: schema-validate, skip analyze-target/scope-definition, write through the canonical writer in place], `[auto]` [optional: bracket modifier passed via pipeline context — when present, BS loads the upstream brief from `brief_path` in pipeline data, enriches it with doc detection, and writes through the canonical writer; requires `brief_path` from AN's `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON`] |
| **Gates** | step 1: Input Gate [use args] | step 3: Confirm Gate [C] | step 4: Confirm Gate [C] |
| **Outputs** | `skill-brief.yaml` at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml`; final `SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON` line on stdout when `{headless_mode}` is true |
| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with heuristic-driven or default action when `{headless_mode}` is true; pre-supplied inputs consumed at the gates that would otherwise prompt; absent `source_authority` and `scope_type` are resolved by signal-driven detection (see `references/headless-args.md`); existing briefs are preserved unless `--force` was supplied (HALT with `overwrite-cancelled` otherwise); supplying `from_brief <path>` instead routes the step 1 GATE to a ratify path that schema-validates the pre-authored brief, skips analyze-target/scope-definition, and writes through the canonical writer (overwriting in place, no `--force` needed) rather than deriving a new brief |
| **Transient-failure retry** | This workflow does **not** auto-retry network or subprocess failures. A failed `gh` fetch (analyze-target.md §1, portfolio-similarity-check.md), QMD probe, or extraction script is logged and surfaced in the final result envelope as a warning, but the workflow continues with whatever signal it has. Headless pipelines that want retry semantics should wrap the invocation at their orchestrator level (e.g. CI re-runner on non-zero exit). Rationale: brief-skill is read-mostly with one terminal write (the YAML at step 5); a partial-signal retry has more failure modes than just re-running the whole workflow, which is cheap. |
| **Exit codes** | See "Exit Codes" below |

## Exit Codes

Every HARD HALT in this workflow exits with a stable code so headless automators can branch on the failure class without grepping message text:

| Code | Meaning              | Raised by                                                                                  |
| ---- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0    | success              | step 6 (terminal)                                                                         |
| 2    | input-missing / input-invalid | step 1 GATE — required headless arg absent (`target_repo`, `skill_name`, or `doc_urls` when `source_type=docs-only`) → `input-missing`; enum violation, malformed semver, non-kebab `skill_name`, or step 5 brief-context schema validation failure → `input-invalid` |
| 3    | resolution-failure   | step 1 §1 (`forge-tier.yaml` missing); step 2 §1 (target inaccessible / `gh auth` fails) |
| 4    | write-failure        | step 1 §1 pre-flight write probe (data folder unwritable: read-only mount, disk full, permissions denied); step 5 §4 (write to `{forge_data_folder}/{skill-name}/skill-brief.yaml` failed) |
| 5    | overwrite-cancelled  | step 5 §2 (existing brief, `force` not supplied)                                          |
| 6    | user-cancelled       | any interactive menu in step 1/03/04 (user selected `[X]` Cancel and exit)                |

## Result Contract (Headless)

When `{headless_mode}` is true, step 5 emits a single-line JSON envelope on **stdout** before chaining to step 6, and every HARD HALT emits the same envelope shape on **stderr** with `status: "error"`:

```
SKF_BRIEF_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success|error","brief_path":"…|null","skill_name":"…","version":"…|null","language":"…|null","scope_type":"…|null","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto|null"}
```

`status` is `"success"` on the terminal happy path, `"error"` on any HALT. `halt_reason` is one of: `null` (success), `"input-missing"`, `"input-invalid"`, `"forge-tier-missing"`, `"target-inaccessible"`, `"gh-auth-failed"`, `"write-failed"`, `"overwrite-cancelled"`, `"user-cancelled"`. `exit_code` matches the table above. `mode` is `"auto"` when BS was invoked with the `[auto]` flag (pipeline auto-brief generation), `null` otherwise (interactive or headless-without-auto).
