1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'write-brief.md'
3	reviseStepFile: 'scope-definition.md'
4	briefSchemaFile: 'assets/skill-brief-schema.md'
5	advancedElicitationSkill: '/bmad-advanced-elicitation'
6	partyModeSkill: '/bmad-party-mode'
7	---
8	
9	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
10	
11	# Step 4: Confirm Brief
12	
13	## STEP GOAL:
14	
15	To present the complete skill brief in human-readable format, highlighting all fields that will be written to skill-brief.yaml, and obtain explicit user approval before writing.
16	
17	## Rules
18	
19	- Focus only on presenting and confirming — do not write files yet (Step 05)
20	- Do not proceed without explicit user approval (P2 confirmation gate)
21	- All user-facing output in `{communication_language}`
22	
23	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
24	
25	### 1. Assemble Complete Brief
26	
27	Use the values already accepted in steps 01-03 directly — do not re-load `{briefSchemaPath}` here. The 18 fields below are all in conversation; the schema is only consulted in §4 if an inline adjustment needs a specific field's validation rule cited.
28	
29	**Ratify run (`ratify_mode: true`):** steps 2-3 were skipped (interactive `[R]` at gather-intent §3.1a, or the headless §8 GATE `from_brief` route), so there is no fresh steps 01-03 output to compile. Use the brief context variables **hydrated from the parsed brief** at step 1 in place of that output — the hydrated variable names match the field references below one-for-one. `detected_version` is absent on this path; rely on the hydrated `version` (step 5 pins it via `version_resolved`).
30	
31	Compile all gathered data from steps 01-03 into the complete brief:
32	
33	- **name:** {skill name from step 01}
34	- **version:** {auto-detected from source, or "1.0.0" if not found — see schema for detection rules}
35	- **target_version:** {target_version from step 01, if set}
36	- **source_repo:** {target repo from step 01}
37	- **language:** {detected/confirmed language from steps 02-03}
38	- **description:** {derived from user intent in step 01}
39	- **forge_tier:** {tier from step 01}
40	- **created:** {current date}
41	- **created_by:** {user_name from config}
42	- **scope.type:** {scope type from step 03}
43	- **scope.include:** {include patterns from step 03}
44	- **scope.exclude:** {exclude patterns from step 03}
45	- **scope.notes:** {any scope notes from step 03}
46	- **scope.tier_a_include:** {tier-A authoring-surface patterns from step 03 §3c — omit if unset}
47	- **scope.rationale:** {recommended -> chosen, reason — from step 03}
48	- **source_type:** {source or docs-only, from step 01}
49	- **doc_urls:** {collected documentation URLs with labels, from steps 01/03 — include if source_type is "docs-only" or supplemental URLs were collected}
50	- **scripts_intent:** {detect/none/description from step 03, or "detect" if not explicitly set}
51	- **assets_intent:** {detect/none/description from step 03, or "detect" if not explicitly set}
52	- **source_authority:** {official/community/internal from step 01 — default "community"}
53	
54	### 2. Present Brief for Review
55	
56	Using the format below:
57	
58	"**Review the complete skill brief before I write it.**
59	
60	---
61	
62	```
63	Skill Brief: {name}
64	====================
65	
66	Target:      {source_repo}
67	Language:    {language}
68	Forge Tier:  {forge_tier} — {tier_gloss}
69	Description: {description}
70	
71	Scope: {scope.type}
72	  Include: {scope.include patterns, one per line}
73	  Exclude: {scope.exclude patterns, one per line}
74	  Notes:   {scope.notes}
75	  Tier-A:  {scope.tier_a_include patterns, one per line — omit this line entirely if scope.tier_a_include unset}
76	  Rationale: {chosen} chosen over {recommended} — {reason}
77	             {omit this line entirely if scope.rationale absent}
78	
79	{If source_type is "docs-only":}
80	Source Type: docs-only
81	Doc URLs:
82	  {doc_urls, one per line with labels}
83	
84	{If source_type is "source" AND supplemental doc_urls collected:}
85	Supplemental Docs:
86	  {doc_urls, one per line with labels}
87	
88	Scripts:    {scripts_intent} — {scripts_gloss}
89	Assets:     {assets_intent} — {assets_gloss}
90	
91	Source Authority: {source_authority}
92	
93	{If target_version is set:}
94	Target Version: {target_version} (user-specified)
95	Detected Version: {detected_version or "N/A"}
96	{Else:}
97	Version:    {version}
98	
99	Created:    {created}
100	Created by: {created_by}
101	```
102	
103	---
104	
105	Glosses (substitute the matching one-liner for `{tier_gloss}`, `{scripts_gloss}`, `{assets_gloss}` above so the user can decode each value at a glance):
106	
107	- **Forge tier glosses** —
108	  - `Quick`: text-only extraction; AST and semantic discovery off
109	  - `Forge`: AST-grep on; semantic and re-ranking off
110	  - `Forge+`: AST-grep + ccc semantic discovery; re-ranking off
111	  - `Deep`: full pipeline — AST + ccc + qmd portfolio search + LLM re-ranking
112	
113	- **`scripts_intent` / `assets_intent` glosses** —
114	  - `detect`: SKF will scan source for the standard `scripts/`/`bin/`/`tools/`/`cli/` (or `assets/`/`templates/`/`schemas/`/`configs/`) directories during create-skill and decide automatically
115	  - `none`: no script/asset packaging — create-skill will skip the detection pass
116	  - free-text (anything else): a description of what to package; create-skill treats it as the user's spec
117	
118	(For `docs-only` and `public-api` scope types the scripts/assets prompt is skipped in step 3 §5b — the values default to `detect` but the create-skill detection pass also no-ops for these scope types, so the gloss just clarifies that the recorded value will not actually fire any scan.)"
119	
120	### 3. Highlight Items Needing Attention
121	
122	Flag any fields that may need review:
123	
124	{If language was overridden or low confidence:}
125	"**Note:** Language was {auto-detected / manually overridden}."
126	
127	"**Description:** synthesized and confirmed in step 1 §7b. This is the text agents read when deciding whether to route to your skill — refine here if you want to tighten it now that the full brief is visible."
128	
129	{If forge tier was defaulted:}
130	"**Note:** Forge tier defaulted to Quick (no forge-tier.yaml found)."
131	
132	{If any scope patterns seem broad or narrow:}
133	"**Note:** {specific observation about scope breadth}."
134	
135	{If target_version is set AND detected_version exists AND they differ:}
136	"**Note:** Target version ({target_version}) differs from detected source version ({detected_version}). The target version will be used for compilation."
137	
138	"**This is your last chance to make changes before writing the file.**
139	
140	You can:
141	- Adjust any field by telling me what to change
142	- Revise scope boundaries by selecting [R]
143	- Proceed to write by selecting [C]"
144	
145	### 4. Handle Inline Adjustments
146	
147	If the user requests changes to specific fields (name, description, version, etc.):
148	- If the adjustment requires explaining a field's validation rule or allowed values, load `{briefSchemaPath}` now (otherwise skip the read — the common path does not need it)
149	- Make the adjustment
150	- Re-present the updated brief
151	- Return to the menu
152	
153	### 5. Present MENU OPTIONS
154	
155	Display: **Select an Option:** [R] Revise Scope [A] Advanced Elicitation [P] Party Mode [C] Approve and Write [X] Cancel and exit
156	
157	#### Menu Handling Logic:
158	
159	- IF R: Load, read entire file, then execute {reviseStepFile} to re-enter scope definition
160	- IF A: Invoke {advancedElicitationSkill}, and when finished redisplay the menu
161	- IF P: Invoke {partyModeSkill}, and when finished redisplay the menu
162	- IF C: Load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
163	- IF X: Treat as user-cancellation. Display `"Cancelled — no brief was written."` and HALT (exit code 6, `halt_reason: "user-cancelled"`). Cancellation here is non-destructive — step 5 has not run, no skill-brief.yaml file exists yet. `[X]` is interactive-only; the headless GATE never reaches this branch.
164	- IF Any other comments or queries: help user respond, apply any field adjustments, re-present brief if changed, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#5-present-menu-options)
165	
166	#### EXECUTION RULES:
167	
168	- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
169	- **GATE [default: C]** — If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [C] Confirm, log: "headless: auto-confirm brief"
170	- ONLY proceed to write step when user selects 'C'
171	- After other menu items execution, return to this menu
172	- User can chat, request field changes, or ask questions — always respond and then redisplay menu
173	
174	## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
175	
176	ONLY WHEN C is selected and the user has explicitly approved the brief will you load and read fully `write-brief.md` to write the skill-brief.yaml file.
177	
178

1	# Headless Gate Convention
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Gates are user interaction points where a workflow pauses for confirmation or input. In headless mode, gates auto-resolve with their default action. This convention ensures one code path with two behaviors — interactive and headless use the same gates, the same output, and the same progression logic.
6	
7	## How It Works
8	
9	Every gate in a step file follows this pattern:
10	
11	```
12	**GATE: [default action]** — Present [options] to user.
13	If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [default action], log: "headless: auto-[action]".
14	```
15	
16	The gate always:
17	1. Prepares the same output (summary, preview, menu) regardless of mode
18	2. In interactive mode: displays the output and waits for user input
19	3. In headless mode: displays the output, logs the auto-action, and proceeds with the default
20	
21	## Resolving `{headless_mode}`
22	
23	`{headless_mode}` is resolved during activation from:
24	1. **Args:** `--headless` or `-H` passed to the skill invocation
25	2. **Preferences:** `headless_mode: true` in `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`
26	3. **Default:** `false`
27	
28	Each workflow's On Activation section resolves this variable alongside other config. The forger passes it through when dispatching to workflows.
29	
30	## Gate Types
31	
32	### Confirm Gate (default: Continue)
33	The most common gate. Presents a summary and asks to continue.
34	- Default action: `[C] Continue`
35	- Headless behavior: auto-continue after displaying summary
36	
37	### Review Gate (default: Approve)
38	Presents compiled output for review before writing.
39	- Default action: `[C] Continue` (approve)
40	- Headless behavior: auto-approve after displaying preview
41	
42	### Input Gate (default: use provided args)
43	Requires user-supplied data (skill name, path, etc.).
44	- Default action: use `{headless_args}` if provided
45	- Headless behavior: consume pre-supplied arguments; halt if missing required input
46	
47	### Choice Gate (default: first safe option)
48	Presents a menu with multiple options (P/I/A, etc.).
49	- Default action: varies per gate (documented in step file)
50	- Headless behavior: auto-select the default, log the choice
51	
52	## Headless Args
53	
54	For skills that require user input (skill name, target path, etc.), headless mode accepts arguments via the invocation. Each skill's Invocation Contract documents its required headless args.
55	
56	Example: `@Ferris QS cocoindex --headless` passes `cocoindex` as the target and skips all gates.
57	
58	## What Headless Does NOT Skip
59	
60	- Error halts (hard halts on missing files, invalid state)
61	- Progress output (summaries, status updates still display)
62	- Quality thresholds (if a step produces output below spec, it still reports the issue)

file.content:
# Headless Gate Convention

## Overview

Gates are user interaction points where a workflow pauses for confirmation or input. In headless mode, gates auto-resolve with their default action. This convention ensures one code path with two behaviors — interactive and headless use the same gates, the same output, and the same progression logic.

## How It Works

Every gate in a step file follows this pattern:

```
**GATE: [default action]** — Present [options] to user.
If `{headless_mode}`: auto-proceed with [default action], log: "headless: auto-[action]".
```

The gate always:
1. Prepares the same output (summary, preview, menu) regardless of mode
2. In interactive mode: displays the output and waits for user input
3. In headless mode: displays the output, logs the auto-action, and proceeds with the default

## Resolving `{headless_mode}`

`{headless_mode}` is resolved during activation from:
1. **Args:** `--headless` or `-H` passed to the skill invocation
2. **Preferences:** `headless_mode: true` in `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`
3. **Default:** `false`

Each workflow's On Activation section resolves this variable alongside other config. The forger passes it through when dispatching to workflows.

## Gate Types

### Confirm Gate (default: Continue)
The most common gate. Presents a summary and asks to continue.
- Default action: `[C] Continue`
- Headless behavior: auto-continue after displaying summary

### Review Gate (default: Approve)
Presents compiled output for review before writing.
- Default action: `[C] Continue` (approve)
- Headless behavior: auto-approve after displaying preview

### Input Gate (default: use provided args)
Requires user-supplied data (skill name, path, etc.).
- Default action: use `{headless_args}` if provided
- Headless behavior: consume pre-supplied arguments; halt if missing required input

### Choice Gate (default: first safe option)
Presents a menu with multiple options (P/I/A, etc.).
- Default action: varies per gate (documented in step file)
- Headless behavior: auto-select the default, log the choice

## Headless Args

For skills that require user input (skill name, target path, etc.), headless mode accepts arguments via the invocation. Each skill's Invocation Contract documents its required headless args.

Example: `@Ferris QS cocoindex --headless` passes `cocoindex` as the target and skips all gates.

## What Headless Does NOT Skip

- Error halts (hard halts on missing files, invalid state)
- Progress output (summaries, status updates still display)
- Quality thresholds (if a step produces output below spec, it still reports the issue)

1	---
2	nextStepFile: 'analyze-target.md'
3	ratifyTargetFile: 'confirm-brief.md'
4	forgeTierFile: '{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml'
5	descriptionVoiceExamplesFile: 'assets/description-voice-examples.md'
6	headlessArgsFile: 'references/headless-args.md'
7	headlessSourceAuthorityDetectionFile: 'references/headless-source-authority-detection.md'

9	draftCheckpointFile: 'references/draft-checkpoint.md'
10	validateBriefInputsProbeOrder:
11	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-inputs.py'
12	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-inputs.py'
13	validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder:
14	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
15	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-brief-schema.py'
16	emitBriefEnvelopeProbeOrder:
17	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
18	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-emit-brief-result-envelope.py'
19	---
20	
21	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
22	
23	# Step 1: Gather Intent
24	
25	## STEP GOAL:
26	
27	To initialize the brief-skill workflow by discovering the forge tier configuration, then gathering the user's target repository, intent, and any upfront scope hints for skill creation.
28	
29	## Rules
30	
31	- Focus only on gathering intent — do not analyze the repo yet (Step 02)
32	- Do not examine source code or list exports in this step
33	- Open-ended discovery facilitation — collect target repo, user intent, scope hints, skill name
34	- All user-facing output in `{communication_language}`
35	
36	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
37	
38	### 1. Discover Forge Tier
39	
40	**Pre-flight write probe.** Before any conversational state accumulates, verify `{forge_data_folder}` is writable. A read-only mount, full disk, or permissions-denied path otherwise only surfaces at step 5's atomic write — by then the user has invested 5–15 minutes. Run a single-byte write-and-remove probe:
41	
42	```bash
43	mkdir -p "{forge_data_folder}" && \
44	  printf 'probe' > "{forge_data_folder}/.skf-write-probe" && \
45	  rm "{forge_data_folder}/.skf-write-probe"
46	```
47	
48	`mkdir -p` succeeds on a pre-existing read-only mount, but the `printf > file` redirect actually attempts a write — that catches read-only, disk-full, and permissions-denied uniformly. **On any non-zero exit:** HALT (exit code 4, `halt_reason: "write-failed"`) — `"**Error:** {forge_data_folder} is not writable: {captured stderr}. Verify the path exists, the mount is writable, and there is free disk space, then re-run."` In headless mode, emit the error envelope per **step 5 §4b** with `halt_reason: "write-failed"` (skill_name is not yet resolved here — use the placeholder convention documented in §4b). On success, continue silently to the forge-tier load below.
49	
50	Attempt to load `{forgeTierFile}`:
51	
52	**If found:**
53	- Read the tier level (quick, forge, forge+, or deep)
54	- Note available tools for scoping guidance later
55	
56	**Apply tier override:** Read `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`. If `tier_override` is set and is a valid tier value (Quick, Forge, Forge+, or Deep), use it instead of the detected tier.
57	
58	**If found but the YAML cannot be parsed (corrupted or truncated):**
59	- Display: "**Cannot read forge-tier.yaml** at `{forgeTierFile}` — the file exists but failed to parse: `{parser error message}`. The setup workflow can rewrite it cleanly. Until then, the brief workflow falls back to **Quick** tier (no extra tools assumed)."
60	- Continue with `tier = "Quick"` and `tools = {}` — do not HALT. Record `tier_source: "fallback-corrupted-config"` for later diagnostics.

63	- "**Cannot proceed.** forge-tier.yaml not found at `{forgeTierFile}`. Run the **setup** workflow first to configure your forge tier (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep)."

If `brief_path` is not available, HARD HALT with exit code 2 (`input-missing`): "**Auto mode requires `brief_path` in pipeline context — AN must run before BS[auto].**"

80	### 2. Welcome and Explain
81	
82	"**Welcome to Brief Skill — the skill scoping workflow.**
83	
84	**Wanted something different?** This workflow *creates* a new brief — a YAML scoping document for a skill that doesn't yet exist. If you meant to compile an existing brief into a skill (`/skf-create-skill`), package one for distribution (`/skf-export-skill`), or just ask SKF a question, type `cancel` at any prompt and run that workflow instead.
85	
86	I'll help you define exactly what to skill and produce a `skill-brief.yaml` that drives the create-skill compilation workflow.
87	
88	We'll work through this together:
89	1. **Now:** Understand what you want to skill and why
90	2. **Next:** Analyze the target repo structure
91	3. **Then:** Define scope boundaries
92	4. **Finally:** Confirm and write the brief
93	
94	{If tier override was applied:}
95	**Your forge tier:** {override tier} (overridden from {original tier}) — {tier_gloss}
96	{Else:}
97	**Your forge tier:** {detected tier} — {tier_gloss}
98	
99	(Substitute `{tier_gloss}` with the matching one-liner so the user knows what the tier label means: `Quick` → "text-only extraction"; `Forge` → "AST-grep on, semantic discovery off"; `Forge+` → "AST-grep + ccc semantic discovery"; `Deep` → "full pipeline — AST + ccc + qmd portfolio search + LLM re-ranking". The tier sets the ceiling for what the downstream create-skill workflow can do; you can re-run setup later to change it.)
100	
101	Let's get started."

103	### 3. Gather Target Repository
104	
105	This section has four sub-flows. Execute exactly one branch — 3.1a *or* 3.2 *or* 3.3 — based on the user's response in 3.1, then end with the shared confirmation (3.1a is terminal for §3 and jumps directly to confirm-brief.md). Do not mix branches.
106	
107	#### 3.1 Collect target
108	
109	"**What repository or documentation do you want to create a skill for?**
110	
111	Provide one of:
112	- A **GitHub URL** (e.g., `https://github.com/org/repo`)
113	- A **local path** (e.g., `/path/to/project`)
114	- **Documentation URLs** for a docs-only skill (e.g., `https://docs.stripe.com/api`) — use this when no source code is available (SaaS, closed-source)
115	- A **path to an existing `skill-brief.yaml`** (file path or a directory containing one) — use this to ratify a brief produced by another workflow (e.g. `skf-analyze-source`) without re-deriving fields
116	
117	Or type `cancel` / `exit` / `[X]` to leave without writing anything.
118	
119	**Target:**"
120	
121	Wait for user response. Branch on the response:
122	
123	- Empty input, `cancel`, `exit`, `[X]`, `q`, or `:q` → Display `"Cancelled — no brief was written."` and HALT (exit code 6, `halt_reason: "user-cancelled"`). Cancellation here is non-destructive — no files have been written yet by step 1. Headless mode never reaches this branch (the GATE in §8 short-circuits the interactive sub-flows).
124	- Path that resolves to an existing `skill-brief.yaml` (file path ending in `skill-brief.yaml` that exists, OR a directory containing a `skill-brief.yaml`) → §3.1a
125	- Documentation URLs only (no source location) → §3.2
126	- GitHub URL or local filesystem path → §3.3
127	- Any other free-form question (e.g. "what is this?", "show me an example", "how does SKF work?") → answer briefly, re-display the prompt
128	
129	#### 3.1a Branch — Ratify existing brief
130	
131	This branch handles the AN→BS handoff: another workflow (typically `skf-analyze-source`) has already produced a `skill-brief.yaml`, and the user wants to review and confirm it without re-running gather-intent / analyze-target / scope-definition. **This §3.1a path is reached only interactively** — it is entered by typing a brief path at the §3.1 prompt, which headless mode never does. The headless equivalent is the §8 GATE `from_brief` route: it consumes a `from_brief` argument, runs the same schema validation, sets the same `ratify_mode`, hydrates from the same parsed payload, and jumps to step 4 exactly as `[R]` does below — see §8. Keep the two paths' hydration in sync.
132	
133	Resolve the path:
134	
135	- If the user's input ends in `skill-brief.yaml` and points at an existing file → that is the brief path.
136	- Otherwise (input was a directory) → the brief path is `<input>/skill-brief.yaml`.
137	
138	**Validate the brief against the schema** before presenting it. Resolve `{validateBriefSchemaHelper}` from `{validateBriefSchemaProbeOrder}` (first existing path wins; HALT if no candidate exists), then:
139	
140	```bash
141	uv run {validateBriefSchemaHelper} <resolved-brief-path>
142	```
143	
144	The script returns JSON `{valid, errors[], warnings[], halt_reason, brief}`. Apply the result:
145	
146	- **`valid: false`** — surface the `errors[]` messages and the `halt_reason` to the user, then re-display the §3.1 prompt for a corrected path (or another target altogether). Do not HALT — the user may simply have pointed at the wrong file. Example: `"**Brief at `{path}` is invalid:** {first error message}. Pick a different brief, or supply a repo / docs URL instead."`
147	- **`valid: true`** — proceed with the parsed `brief` payload.
148	
149	Surface any non-empty `warnings[]` as a single grouped line (`"**Brief validation warnings:** {joined warnings}"`), then present the ratify menu:
150	
151	```
152	**Existing brief detected at `{path}`.**
153	
154	- **Name:** {brief.name}
155	- **Target:** {brief.source_repo}
156	- **Description:** "{brief.description}"
157	- **Created:** {brief.created} by {brief.created_by}
158	- **Scope:** {brief.scope.type}
159	
160	Pick one:
161	  [R] Ratify — review in step 4 and write (overwriting this file once approved)
162	  [F] Start fresh — discard this brief and re-prompt for a target
163	  [X] Cancel and exit
164	```
165	
166	Wait for user response. Branch:
167	
168	- **[R] Ratify** — Confirm overwrite up front: store `ratify_mode: true` and `ratify_source_path: <resolved-brief-path>` in workflow context. Hydrate the brief context variables from the parsed `brief` payload so step 4 has the same field set it normally derives from steps 1-3:
169	  - `name` ← `brief.name`; `version` ← `brief.version`; `target_version` ← `brief.target_version`
170	  - `target_ref` ← `brief.target_ref`; `source_ref` ← `brief.source_ref` (optional git refs; preserve when present)
171	  - `source_repo` ← `brief.source_repo`; `source_type` ← `brief.source_type`; `source_authority` ← `brief.source_authority`; `doc_urls` ← `brief.doc_urls`
172	  - `language` ← `brief.language`; `description` ← `brief.description`; `forge_tier` ← `brief.forge_tier`
173	  - `created` ← `brief.created`; `created_by` ← `brief.created_by`
174	  - `scope.type` / `scope.include` / `scope.exclude` / `scope.tier_a_include` / `scope.notes` / `scope.rationale` / `scope.amendments` ← `brief.scope.*` (preserve `tier_a_include` and the `amendments` log verbatim — do not re-derive or drop them)
175	  - `scripts_intent` ← `brief.scripts_intent`; `assets_intent` ← `brief.assets_intent`
176	
177	  Then load, read entirely, and execute `{ratifyTargetFile}` — bypassing §3.1b/§3.2/§3.3, §3b, §4, §5, §6, §7, §7b, and §8 entirely. Skip step 2 (analyze-target) and step 3 (scope-definition) — both would re-derive fields already on disk. The forward chain resumes at step 4 (confirm-brief) where the user gets the standard review pass and can still adjust fields inline via §4.
178	
179	- **[F] Start fresh** — discard the loaded brief and re-display §3.1 above (the user is now at the same point as if they had typed nothing).
180	- **[X] Cancel** — Display `"Cancelled — no brief was written."` and HALT (exit code 6, `halt_reason: "user-cancelled"`). Non-destructive.
181	- **Any other input** — treat as a fresh §3.1 response and re-evaluate the routing branches above (a typed GitHub URL after seeing the menu means "I changed my mind, brief this repo instead").
182	
183	#### 3.2 Branch — Documentation URLs (docs-only)
184	
185	- Set `source_type: "docs-only"` in the brief data
186	- Collect one or more doc URLs with optional labels
187	- HEAD-check the collected URLs in parallel — do not loop sequentially. Issue all N `curl -sI {url}` (or equivalent) calls in a **single message with N parallel Bash calls**, then process the responses together. Each call must use a 5-second timeout (`curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}`) to bound worst-case wall-time on hung hosts. Per response:
188	  - On 2xx/3xx: silently accept.
189	  - On 4xx/5xx, DNS failure, or timeout: warn `"Could not reach {url} — {status or error}. Confirm the URL is correct, or proceed anyway."` Interactive: re-prompt for a corrected URL or `[K] Keep anyway`. Headless: keep the URL and log the warning — the brief still records it but the failure is now visible at brief-creation time instead of materializing hours later in skf-create-skill.
190	- Set `source_authority: "community"` (forced for docs-only — T3 external documentation; the §3.3 source-authority prompt is skipped)
191	- Note: `source_repo` becomes optional (can be set to the main doc site URL for reference)
192	
193	Skip §3.3 and continue at "Confirm the target" below.
194	
195	#### 3.3 Branch — Source (GitHub URL or local path)
196	
197	- Set `source_type: "source"` (default)
198	- **Pre-validate the target before continuing.** Issue these probes in a single message with parallel Bash calls:
199	  - **GitHub URL:** `curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}`. On a 4xx (typically 404 for a typo'd repo or org), warn `"GitHub returned {status} for {url} — confirm the URL is correct."` and re-prompt. On 2xx, accept.
200	  - **GitHub URL, in parallel:** `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo} --jq .name` (5-second timeout via `gh api --hostname github.com --method GET ... ` or just rely on default). On 403/404, warn `"GitHub API returned {status} for {owner}/{repo} — the repo may be private or your token may not have access. Step-02 will HALT here if this is not resolved. Continue anyway, or fix and re-prompt?"` and offer `[K] Keep anyway` / re-prompt for a corrected URL. Do not HALT — the canonical HALT still happens in step 2 §1, but surfacing access failures at URL-entry time prevents 5+ minutes of intent investment getting lost. On any other error (network failure, missing binary), log silently and let `gh auth status` below catch it. On 2xx, accept silently.
201	  - **GitHub URL, in parallel:** `gh auth status` — if it reports unauthenticated or the binary is missing, warn `"GitHub CLI not authenticated; step 2 will HALT when it tries to fetch the tree. Run 'gh auth login' before continuing, or supply a local clone path instead."` (Do not HALT here — let the user choose to fix or proceed; the canonical HALT still happens in step 2 §1's failure-class triage.)
202	  - **Local path:** verify the directory exists (`test -d {path}`). If not, warn `"Local path {path} does not exist."` and re-prompt.
203	- Optionally ask: "Are there any documentation URLs you'd like to include for supplemental context? (These will be fetched as T3 external references.)"
204	- If yes: collect doc URLs into `doc_urls`
205	
206	**Source authority (this branch only — docs-only forces `community` in §3.2):**
207	
208	**Interactive only** — skip this prompt entirely when `{headless_mode}` is true; the GATE in §8 resolves source_authority headlessly via the detection branch documented there.
209	
210	"**Are you the maintainer of this library, or creating a community skill?**"
211	- If maintainer: set `source_authority: "official"`
212	- If community user: set `source_authority: "community"` (default)
213	- If internal/proprietary: set `source_authority: "internal"`
214	
215	Default to `"community"` if user does not specify or skips.
216	
217	---
218	
219	Confirm the target.

221	### 3b. Gather Target Version
222	
223	This step only collects `target_version` and validates its shape with the regex below — auto-detection runs in step 2 and precedence/invariant resolution lands in step 5's writer script. The canonical precedence rules live in `references/version-resolution.md`; load it from step 2 / step 5 only when the relevant section needs it.
224	
225	**Headless:** if `target_version` was supplied as an argument, store it and skip the interactive prompt below. If `doc_urls` were also supplied, treat the version-vs-doc-URL confirmation prompt as auto-confirmed (Y).
226	
227	"**Are you targeting a specific version of this library?**
228	(Leave blank to auto-detect from source)"
229	
230	{If source_type is "docs-only":}
231	"Since this is a docs-only skill with no source code, specifying the version is recommended — otherwise it defaults to 1.0.0."
232	
233	Wait for user response.
234	
235	**If user provides a version:** Validate the shape against `^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+([.\-+][0-9A-Za-z][0-9A-Za-z.\-+]*)?$` (full X.Y.Z form, with optional `v` prefix and pre-release / build suffix; CalVer like `2024.04.01` accepted; partial forms like `1`, `1.2`, `v2`, `latest` rejected). On a match, store as `target_version` and set `version` to this value. On a non-match, warn `"'{value}' doesn't look like semver — write the explicit triple (e.g. 1.0.0). Fix it now or skip auto-detection?"` and re-prompt for a corrected value or blank to fall through to step 2 auto-detection.
236	**If blank:** Proceed without `target_version` — version will be auto-detected in step 02.
237	
238	{If target_version was set AND doc_urls are being collected (either docs-only primary or supplemental):}
239	
240	"**You're targeting version {target_version}. Do these documentation URLs correspond to that version?** [Y/N]"
241	
242	- **If Y:** Proceed.
243	- **If N:** "Provide the correct documentation URLs for version {target_version}." Re-collect doc_urls.

246	
247	**First-timer rail (interactive only).** Before the intent prompt, check whether `{forge_data_folder}/` contains any prior briefs:

250	find "{forge_data_folder}" -maxdepth 2 -name "skill-brief.yaml" -print -quit
251	```
252	
253	If the command produces any output, skip this rail silently — repeat users don't need the warm-up. If it produces no output (the user has never produced a brief), ask:

256	
257	On `[Y]`: load `{descriptionVoiceExamplesPath}` and present the five examples verbatim with a one-line preface (`"Each example shows a different voice — yours doesn't have to match any specific one."`). On `[N]` or empty: proceed silently.
258	
259	"**What's your intent for this skill?**
260	
261	Help me understand:
262	- **What** specifically do you want to skill from this repo?
263	- **Why** — what's the use case? How will an AI agent use this skill?
264	- **Any initial thoughts** on scope? (Full library? Specific modules? Public API only?)
265	
266	Take your time — the more context you share, the better the brief."
267	
268	Wait for user response. Ask follow-up questions if intent is unclear.

271	
272	If the user mentioned scope preferences in their intent response, acknowledge them:
273	
274	"**I noted these scope hints from your response:**
275	- {list any scope hints mentioned}
276	
277	We'll refine these after analyzing the repo structure in the next step."
278	
279	If no scope hints were mentioned, that's fine — skip this acknowledgment.

281	### 6. Derive Skill Name
282	
283	Based on the target repo and intent, propose a skill name:
284	
285	"**Suggested skill name:** `{derived-name}` (kebab-case)
286	
287	This will be used for the output directory and file naming. Want to use this name or suggest something different?"
288	
289	Wait for confirmation or alternative.
290	
291	**Collision check (interactive and headless):** before locking the name, check whether `{forge_data_folder}/{name}/skill-brief.yaml` already exists. If it does:
292	
293	- Interactive: generate 1–3 non-colliding candidate alternates by scanning sibling directories under `{forge_data_folder}/`. Apply each rule that fires; skip rules whose precondition isn't met:
294	  1. `{name}-v{N}` where `N` is the smallest positive integer that doesn't collide (e.g. `{name}-v2`, `{name}-v3`) — always applies
295	  2. `{name}-{target_version}` if `target_version` is set and the suffix wouldn't collide (e.g. `marked-1.2.3`)
296	  3. `{name}-{source_authority}` if `source_authority` is not `community` (e.g. `marked-internal` for an internal fork)
297	
298	  Number the surviving alternates `[1] [2] [3]…` in the order produced (1 alternate for a community-authority brief with no `target_version`; 2–3 otherwise). Then present:
299	
300	  ```
301	  **Heads up — a brief for `{name}` already exists at `{path}`.**
302	
303	  Suggested alternates (none collide):
304	    [1] {alternate-1}
305	    {if a second alternate was produced:} [2] {alternate-2}
306	    {if a third alternate was produced:} [3] {alternate-3}
307	
308	  Pick a number to use that name, type a different name, or press Enter to keep `{name}` and let step 5 §2b handle the overwrite prompt.
309	  ```
310	
311	  On a numbered choice, replace `{name}` with the chosen alternate. On Enter, fall through to step 5's overwrite gate. On any other input, treat as a new candidate name and re-run the collision check against it.
312	
313	- Headless: log `"warn: skill name '{name}' collides with existing brief at {path}"` and proceed; the existing-brief overwrite policy in step 5 §2b is the canonical gate (HALT with `overwrite-cancelled` unless `force` was supplied).
314	
315	**Portfolio-similarity check.** When the flow is interactive AND forge tier is `Deep` AND `tools.qmd` is true in `forge-tier.yaml`, load `{portfolioSimilarityCheckFile}` and follow the procedure there to catch semantic near-duplicates that exact-name collision misses. Otherwise (headless, or tier below Deep, or qmd unavailable) skip the load — the check does not run.
316	
317	**Draft-resume check.** When the flow is interactive AND `{forge_data_folder}/{name}/.brief-draft.json` exists AND no `skill-brief.yaml` sits beside it, load `{draftCheckpointFile}` and follow Half 1 (Resume Check). On `[Y]` resume, the procedure jumps directly to §8 with prior answers restored — **the rest of §6, all of §7, and all of §7b are skipped**. Otherwise (headless, or no draft file, or a finished brief sits beside the draft) skip the load and continue with §6 normally.

320	
321	"**Here's what I've captured:**
322	
323	- **Target:** {repo URL or path}
324	- **Intent:** {user's intent summary}
325	- **Scope hints:** {any hints, or "None — we'll define scope after analysis"}
326	- **Skill name:** {confirmed name}
327	- **Source type:** {source or docs-only}
328	- **Source authority:** {official/community/internal}

333	- **Forge tier:** {tier}

335	Ready to analyze the target repository?"
